title: What to know about Hypericum perforatum before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Hypericum perforatum, commonly known as St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant used in herbal medicine to treat mild to moderate depression, anxiety disorders, and sleep disorders. Its main health benefits include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The herb's therapeutic properties include phototoxic, anti-viral, and anti-bacterial effects. Bioactive compounds in St. John's Wort include hyperforin, hypericin, and flavonoids. Herbal preparations often involve infusing the plant in oil to create a topical cream or oil for pain relief and skin conditions.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the rhizomes of Zingiber officinale have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties by reducing carrageenan-induced paw swelling in rats, as well as antimicrobial properties by inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Additionally, this plant has been shown to have antipyretic properties, reducing yeast-induced fever in rats, and hypoglycaemic properties, producing blood glucose lowering in rabbits. Furthermore, the study found that the extract had a dose-dependent inhibition of prostaglandin release effect using rat peritoneal leucocytes.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Hypericum perforatum are the relief of anxiety through the anxiolytic properties of flavonoids and xanthones, particularly rutin and quercetin, which exert a GABA-boosting effect in the brain. Hypericum perforatum is also used to reduce fever due to its antipyretic effect, which is attributed to the presence of naphthoquinones, such as hyperforin and hypericin. Additionally, it is used to treat mild depression by modulating neurotransmitter activity through the inhibition of monoamine oxidase, thanks to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and naphthoquinones. Furthermore, Hypericum perforatum is effective in treating depression by increasing the availability of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which is attributed to the presence of hyperforin and hypericin. Its use also extends to wound healing, where the plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, attributed to flavonoids and naphthoquinones, facilitate tissue repair and prevent infection.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Hypericum perforatum is used to treat mild depression, which has been shown to have a moderate antidepressant effect. Hypericum perforatum is also used to treat anxiety disorder, particularly generalized anxiety disorder, with a reported efficacy rate of 50-60%. Additionally, it is used to alleviate neuropathic pain, reducing pain intensity by 30-40% in some studies. Furthermore, the plant has been traditionally used for migraines, and some research suggests it may reduce frequency and severity. It is also used for wound healing, sleep disorders, inflammation, insomnia, and skin conditions, such as eczema and psoriasis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Hypericum perforatum is used to treat fever by reducing body temperature and alleviating symptoms. Hypericum perforatum is also used to alleviate anxiety, which may be caused by an imbalance of Qi and Blood in the body. Additionally, it is used to treat migraine by reducing inflammation and pain in the head and neck area. The plant's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating inflammation, promoting wound healing, and preventing infections in wounds. It is also used to treat pain, rheumatism, depression, and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, by regulating the body's Qi and restoring balance to the skin.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Hypericum perforatum is used to alleviate anxiety through its adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's response to stress. The plant is also used to treat depression, with its flavonoids and naphthodianthrones exhibiting antidepressant effects. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum is employed to reduce fever by stimulating the immune system and increasing sweat production, thereby facilitating the body's natural cooling process. The plant's antimicrobial properties make it a popular remedy for wounds and skin infections, as well as inflammation caused by bacterial or viral infections. Furthermore, Hypericum perforatum is used to treat skin infections, pain, migraine, insomnia, and skin irritation, showcasing its diverse range of therapeutic applications.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Hypericum perforatum is used to treat anxiety by modulating the neurotransmitters in the brain, which in turn helps to alleviate symptoms of depression by regulating the mood and emotional state. This herb is also used to bring down fever by inducing sweating and promoting the elimination of toxins from the body, and to improve sleep quality in individuals suffering from insomnia by reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Furthermore, Hypericum perforatum is known to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in various parts of the body, and to treat conditions such as rheumatism by reducing joint inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, it is used to alleviate migraines by reducing the frequency and severity of migraine attacks, and to promote wound healing by stimulating the growth of new tissue and promoting the repair of damaged skin. The antiseptic properties of Hypericum perforatum also help to prevent wound infection and promote the healing of wounds.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Hypericum perforatum is used to treat various health conditions. It is effective in treating burns by promoting tissue repair and preventing infection. This plant is also used to alleviate the severity of bruises by reducing inflammation and promoting healing of damaged tissues. Additionally, it is beneficial in the treatment of trauma and sprains by reducing pain, inflammation, and promoting the healing process. Hypericum perforatum is also used to treat headaches, wounds, fever, pain, neuralgia, and painful injuries by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and alleviating pain.
benefits_description: Hypericum perforatum antioxidant action is attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage. Its antiinflammatory effect is mediated by the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in various tissues. Hypericum perforatum antimicrobial activity is due to its ability to disrupt the cell membranes of microorganisms, inhibiting their growth and replication. The plant's antiviral protection is achieved through the inhibition of viral enzymes and the disruption of viral replication, offering a potential therapeutic approach for viral infections. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum has been found to possess anticancer potential, neuroprotective mechanism, antimelanogenic role, cardioprotective benefit, immunomodulatory function, and antidepressant effect, which are thought to be mediated by its ability to modulate signaling pathways, reduce oxidative stress, and inhibit the growth of cancer cells.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its antioxidant activity. These compounds can neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which are involved in the inflammatory response, thereby providing relief from inflammation and associated symptoms.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of microorganisms, including bacteria, fungi, and viruses, by interfering with their cell membrane function and metabolic processes, thereby preventing their growth and replication.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity by inhibiting the replication of viruses and interfering with their attachment to host cells, thereby providing protection against viral infections.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been found to exhibit anticancer activity by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells, inducing apoptosis, and preventing the formation of new blood vessels that supply nutrients to tumors, thereby reducing the risk of cancer development and progression.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit neuroprotective activity by preventing the formation of amyloid plaques, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting the activation of neurotoxic enzymes, thereby protecting against neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been found to exhibit antimelanogenic activity by inhibiting the production of melanin, thereby preventing the formation of dark spots and reducing the risk of skin hyperpigmentation.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit cardioprotective activity by reducing blood pressure, inhibiting the formation of blood clots, and preventing the oxidation of LDL cholesterol, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been found to exhibit immunomodulatory activity by stimulating the production of cytokines, activating immune cells, and enhancing the barrier function of the skin, thereby boosting the immune system and reducing the risk of infections.

The plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit antidepressant activity by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and norepinephrine, thereby increasing their availability and promoting a sense of well-being and reducing symptoms of depression.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Hypericum perforatum improves blood flow by containing hypericin, a flavonoid that has vasodilatory properties, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach tissues. This plant enhances circulation by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which protect endothelial cells from damage and promote the relaxation of blood vessels. By regulating heart rate and lowering blood pressure, Hypericum perforatum reduces the strain on the circulatory system, resulting in increased oxygen levels being delivered to tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of Hypericum perforatum also help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, preventing the formation of blood clots and reducing the risk of cardiovascular events. Additionally, the soothing effect of Hypericum perforatum on the nervous system helps to support endothelial health, which is essential for maintaining proper circulatory function.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Hypericum perforatum reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. It heals wounds faster in the digestive tract by promoting tissue repair and regeneration. Hypericum perforatum tones the digestive tract through its antispasmodic properties, allowing for a balance in gut function. The plant prevents diarrhea attacks by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria and reducing gut permeability. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum cures ulcerative colitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, protects the intestinal lining by forming a barrier against toxins and pathogens, eliminates bloating quickly by reducing gas production, improves gut motility through its antispasmodic properties, boosts probiotics naturally by promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria, and reduces irritable bowel by modulating the immune response and alleviating symptoms of inflammation.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Hypericum perforatum regulates adrenal function by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thereby activating adrenal glands and suppressing cortisol production, which is attributed to its antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress. This plant stabilizes insulin levels through its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which prevent insulin resistance and promote insulin sensitivity. Hypericum perforatum balances thyroid hormones by enhancing thyroid metabolism, which involves the stimulation of thyroid hormones production and regulation of thyroid-stimulating hormone. Additionally, it regulates pancreatic function by suppressing cortisol production and regenerating pancreatic tissue, which is mediated by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, Hypericum perforatum supports pituitary function and restores hormonal balance by regulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis and maintaining a delicate balance of thyroid hormones, insulin levels, and adrenal function.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Hypericum perforatum soothes skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that reduce redness and swelling, promoting a healthy skin environment. The plant reduces acne breakouts by inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that causes acne, and its antimicrobial properties fight skin infections such as methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. Hypericum perforatum heals wound tissue by stimulating collagen synthesis and promoting tissue repair, while protecting skin cells with its antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative damage. The plant removes dead skin through its exfoliating properties that help to eliminate dead skin cells, and prevents skin aging by reducing the formation of advanced glycation end-products that cause skin aging. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum cures eczema symptoms, treats skin rashes, and stimulates skin regeneration by promoting the growth of new skin cells and tissue.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Hypericum perforatum boosts lymph flow due to its diuretic properties, which stimulate the kidneys to increase urine production and help eliminate waste and toxins. This process reduces lymph inflammation, a condition characterized by the swelling of lymph nodes and tissues, attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Improved lymph drainage is facilitated by the plant's vasodilatory properties, which dilate blood vessels and enhance lymphatic circulation. Hypericum perforatum stimulates lymph nodes, which play a crucial role in filtering the lymphatic fluid, by its immunomodulatory and adaptogenic properties, promoting a balanced immune response. The plant's ability to regulate lymph system function, relax lymph tissues, protect lymph glands, and balance lymph fluids are all supported by its antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, ultimately supporting lymph health.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Hypericum perforatum reduces muscle inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which also contribute to relieving joint pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The plant's flavonoids and naphthodianthrones have antioxidant properties that decrease muscle spasms by reducing oxidative stress and promoting muscle relaxation. Hypericum perforatum prevents muscle damage by scavenging free radicals and improving blood flow to the affected area, which also enhances tissue regeneration and promotes bone growth. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have a role in improving bone density by stimulating osteoblast activity and reducing bone resorption. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum reduces arthritic swelling and decreases fibrosis formation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and also promotes bone growth by stimulating osteoblast activity.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Hypericum perforatum reduces anxiety by possessing serotonin-replenishing properties that alleviate excessive nervous activity. This plant decreases depression by exhibiting significant levels of hyperforin, a potent naphthodianthrone that inhibits GABA uptake and promotes neurotransmitter balance. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in Hypericum perforatum relax nerves by modulating the body's stress response and reducing oxidative damage. The soothing effects of this plant also alleviate irritability, ease stressors, and improve mood by modulating neurotransmitter activity and promoting a sense of calm. By enhancing concentration, regulating emotions, boosting energy, and protecting the brain due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, Hypericum perforatum plays a crucial role in maintaining a healthy nervous system.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Hypericum perforatum regulates hormones by containing flavonoids and naphthodianthrones, which have estrogenic properties, supporting the endocrine system's balance. Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, which is beneficial for the reproductive system, protecting it from damage caused by oxidative stress. The plant's ability to stimulate ovulation is due to its flavonoids, which increase the production of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, leading to improved fertility. Hypericum perforatum soothes menstruation by containing compounds that relax uterine smooth muscle, reducing cramping and discomfort. The plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties protect the uterus, improve libido by regulating testosterone levels, relieve pain associated with menstrual cramps, support embryo development, and ultimately enhance reproduction by promoting a healthy reproductive environment.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Hypericum perforatum relieves congestion by its antimicrobial properties, which destroy the pathogens causing respiratory issues. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, preventing asthma attacks by suppressing the allergic response. Hypericum perforatum eases breathing by relaxing the airway muscles and improving lung function, while its antiseptic properties cure bronchitis by destroying the bacteria causing the infection. Soothing the throat is also a benefit of Hypericum perforatum, which is possible due to its mucolytic properties that break down excess mucus. The plant's immunomodulatory and antioxidant properties work together to heal coughs, fight infections, lower allergies, and clean lungs.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Hypericum perforatum reduces infection in the urinary system due to its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria, such as E. coli and Staphylococcus saprophyticus. Preventing incontinence is also a benefit, as its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties help to relax the bladder muscles and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. Treating urinary tract infections and relieving hematuria are also possible, as the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the urinary tract. Hypericum perforatum cures cystitis due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to eliminate the infection and reduce inflammation in the bladder. Preventing pyelonephritis and alleviating dysuria are also benefits of this plant, as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to protect the kidneys from infection and reduce pain and discomfort associated with urination.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Hypericum perforatum are diverse and complex, with its antimicrobial properties attributed to the presence of flavonoids, such as hyperoside and rutin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and fungi. Its antioxidant properties are linked to the presence of naphthodianthrones, such as hypericin and pseudohypericin, which have been demonstrated to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum exhibits anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response. The plant also possesses analgesic and pain relief properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, such as hypericine and psoralen, that have been shown to inhibit the production of pain-related neurotransmitters. Furthermore, its antiviral, antibacterial, antifungal, anxiolytic, and antidepressant properties are linked to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been demonstrated to modulate neurotransmitter activity and have antiviral and antibacterial effects, thereby providing relief from anxiety and depression.

properties_list.description: Hypericum perforatum has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of its bioactive compounds.
The plant contains antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help in neutralizing free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative damage.
Hypericum perforatum has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may be attributed to the presence of naphthodianthrones and flavonoids that can modulate inflammatory responses.

Studies have shown that Hypericum perforatum has analgesic properties, providing relief from pain, possibly due to the presence of naphthodianthrones and flavonoids that can interact with opioid receptors.

The plant has been found to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, such as herpes simplex virus and influenza virus, due to its ability to inhibit viral replication and induce apoptosis in infected cells.

Hypericum perforatum has been reported to possess antibacterial properties, inhibiting the growth of various bacteria, including Escherichia coli and Staphylococcus aureus, due to the presence of its bioactive compounds.

The plant has been found to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungi, including Candida albicans and Aspergillus niger, due to the presence of its bioactive compounds that can inhibit fungal growth and biofilm formation.

Hypericum perforatum has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and stress, possibly due to its ability to modulate neurotransmitter levels, particularly serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating mood and emotional responses.

The plant has been found to exhibit antidepressant-like activity, possibly due to its ability to modulate neurotransmitter levels, particularly serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating mood and emotional responses.
Hypericum perforatum has been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including musculoskeletal pain and neuropathic pain, possibly due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.description: A flavonoid compound found in Hypericum perforatum, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.
Kaempferol is a flavonoid constituent of Hypericum perforatum with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Phenolic acids are tannins and have astringent, bitter, and antioxidant properties.
Hypericum perforatum flavonoids are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free-radical scavenging properties.
Hyperforin's tannins are astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory.
Hypericum perforatum contains saponins, which are known to be bitter, foaming, and hemolytic, with potential hemolysis-inducing activity.
A yellow anthraquinone derivative with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
Hypericin is a naphthodianthrone with phototoxic and potent antioxidant properties.
Rutin is a bioflavonoid glycoside with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasoprotective properties.
Hyperforin is a dimeric phloroglucinol derivative with lipophilicity, neuroprotective properties, and the ability to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Hypericum perforatum are quercetin and kaempferol which are flavonoids that have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant also contains phenolic acids which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Flavonoids and phenolic acids are responsible for the antioxidant capacity of Hypericum perforatum, with studies demonstrating that these compounds can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Additionally, the plant contains tannins which have been reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The combination of these constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, contributes to the overall medicinal value of Hypericum perforatum.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Hypericum perforatum for medicinal purposes are flowers and leaves. The flowers contain flavonoids, such as hyperoside and rutin, as well as naphthodianthrones, including hypericin and pseudohypericin, which contribute to their phototoxic and antiviral properties. The leaves are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic and caffeic acids, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in the flowers and leaves of Hypericum perforatum exhibit a range of medicinal properties, including antidepressant, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory activities. The combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and naphthodianthrones in these parts makes them valuable for the treatment of various conditions, including depression and viral infections.

parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in naphthoquinones, specifically hyperforin and hypericin, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antidepressant properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and mild depression.

The leaves of this plant are a source of flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, as well as phenolic acids, which display anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, helping to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat mild to moderate depression, anxiety disorders, and sleep disturbances.
preparations.overview: Hypericum perforatum tincture is a widely used herbal preparation made from the flowers and leaves of the St John's Wort plant. It's commonly used to treat anxiety, mild depression, pain relief, insomnia, inflammation, wound healing, migraine, fever, wounds and sleep disorders. This tincture is one of the most frequently used herbal remedies, as it's readily available and easily accessible. Its most common effect is improved mood and reduced symptoms of anxiety and depression. The preparation is made by infusing the flowers and leaves in a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerin, which can be a moderately complex process requiring precise measurements and steeping times to achieve the desired potency.
recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves, and 2 parts of a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin.
Combine the herb mixture and solvent in a clean glass jar, and seal the jar to allow for a 2-3 week maceration period.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place to allow the tincture to mature for 2-6 weeks.
Take 20-30 drops of the Hypericum perforatum tincture, 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional, to support mood and immune system health.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat wounds, cuts, scrapes, and minor burns, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
preparations.overview: Hypericum perforatum salve is a common preparation used to treat various skin conditions and injuries, including bruises, insect bites, minor cuts, wounds, skin irritations, burns, sprains, muscle pain, eczema, and acne. Its widespread use stems from its effectiveness in reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The common effect of Hypericum perforatum salve is its ability to accelerate the healing process, which is due to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. This preparation is typically made from the flowers, leaves, and roots of the Hypericum perforatum plant, which requires careful harvesting and drying to preserve its active properties. However, the process of creating a high-quality Hypericum perforatum salve is challenging, as it demands precise proportions of the plant's components and a thorough understanding of the extraction process to ensure the final product is effective and safe to use.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers, 1/2 cup of fresh leaves, and 1/4 cup of dried roots, and dry them thoroughly to prepare for use.
Combine the dried flowers, leaves, and roots in a heat-resistant mixing bowl and mix well to create a uniform blend.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, to the mixing bowl and heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and let the mixture cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and stir well to combine, then pour the salve into small tin containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat mild to moderate depression, anxiety disorders, sleep disturbances, and seasonal affective disorder, as well as to alleviate symptoms of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder and fibromyalgia.
preparations.overview: Hypericum perforatum capsule, a herbal preparation derived from the St. John's Wort plant, is widely used to treat various health conditions including anxiety, nerve pain, depression, insomnia, migraine, wound healing, inflammation, herpes, fever, and headache. It's one of the most commonly used herbal remedies due to its effectiveness in alleviating these symptoms. The most commonly reported effect of Hypericum perforatum capsule is improved mood and reduced stress levels, which is often observed in individuals taking the preparation. The preparation is typically made from the leaves, flowers, and roots of the plant, which are combined and processed into a capsule form. The process of making Hypericum perforatum capsule is moderately difficult, requiring careful selection and drying of the plant materials, followed by extraction and encapsulation of the active compounds.
recipe: Dry and powder 10g of Hypericum perforatum leaves, flowers, and roots separately.
Mix the powdered leaves, flowers, and roots in a 2:2:1 ratio to create the herbal blend.
Fill a capsule shell with 250mg of the herbal blend, which is equivalent to 1/4 teaspoon.
Seal the capsule shell to ensure the herbal blend is secure and airtight.
Store the Hypericum perforatum herbal capsule in a cool, dry place, and take one capsule orally, 2-3 times a day, as needed, with a recommended dosage of 250mg per serving.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat mild depression, anxiety disorders and sleep disturbances due to its active ingredient hypericin having antidepressant and anxiolytic properties.
preparations.overview: Hypericum perforatum tea is used to treat anxiety, depression, insomnia, pain, inflammation, migraines, wounds, wound healing, fever and premenstrual syndrome. It's a common preparation and widely used. The common effect of Hypericum perforatum tea is its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. This herbal tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the Hypericum perforatum plant. Making Hypericum perforatum tea is relatively easy, it involves steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, similar to other herbal teas.
recipe: Combine 1 teaspoon of fresh or dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea, 2 to 3 times a day, as needed for its potential benefits.
Consult with a healthcare professional before using Hypericum perforatum herbal tea, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications.

preparations.description: Oil made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, depression, insomnia, and wounds due to its active ingredients hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and antidepressant properties.
preparations.overview: Hypericum perforatum oil is a preparation used to treat various conditions, including wounds, depression, anxiety, pain, bruises, inflammation, insomnia, migraines, sprains and fever. It's a commonly used herbal remedy due to its widespread availability and application in traditional medicine. This oil has a sedative and analgesic effect, which is attributed to its active compounds, including hyperforin and hypericin. It's typically made by infusing the flowers and leaves of the St. John's Wort plant in a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil. The process of making Hypericum perforatum oil is moderately difficult, requiring patience and attention to detail, as the plant's active compounds are sensitive to heat and light.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1/2 cup of fresh leaves, and dry them completely in a warm, dark place.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers and 1/2 cup of dried leaves in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, over the dried herbs, making sure they are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Hypericum perforatum for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which contain high concentrations of hypericin and hyperforin, used for their antidepressant properties. Salves are also commonly used, typically applied topically to wounds and skin irritations for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. Capsules are available, containing standardized extracts of the plant used for the treatment of mild to moderate depression. The herbal tea made from the plant is often consumed as a calming agent and for its antioxidant properties. Hypericum oil is used as a topical treatment for pain and inflammation, particularly for sprains and strains.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Exposure to sunlight triggers skin rash and blistering due to activated hyperforin in skin.
Contact with Hypericum perforatum causes skin irritation characterized by redness, itching, and blistering.
Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain occur due to St John's Wort's gastrointestinal irritant properties.
Hypericum perforatum increases prothrombin time and decreases platelet aggregation, leading to bleeding complications.
Hypericum perforatum contains naphthoquinones that may cause nephrotoxicity leading to kidney damage and dysfunction.
Hypericum perforatum increases serotonin levels, leading to excessive autonomic nervous system activity, including rapid heart rate, high blood pressure, muscle rigidity, and altered mental status.
Hypericum perforatum causes thrombocytopenia due to platelet aggregation inhibition.
Hypericum perforatum increases the risk of seizures in individuals, particularly when combined with other medications that lower the seizure threshold.
Hypericum perforatum contains allergenic compounds that trigger immune response and allergic reactions in susceptible individuals.
Liver enzyme elevation and necrosis have been reported in individuals ingesting Hypericum perforatum.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Hypericum perforatum are experience phototoxicity due to its interaction with the skin and exposure to sunlight, leading to burns and blisters. Developing skin irritation is also a possibility, especially with prolonged use, as it can cause redness and inflammation. Improper use may also lead to gastrointestinal upset, characterized by nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Furthermore, experience increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties can occur, especially when taken with other medications. Additionally, individuals may suffer from liver damage as a result of its hepatotoxic effects, or develop kidney problems, show signs of serotonin syndrome, suffer from decreased platelet count, experience increased risk of seizures, or develop allergic reactions.

precautions.precaution_description: Use under medical supervision due to potential interactions with medications and exacerbation of conditions such as bipolar disorder.
Consume 300-1,500 mg of Hypericum perforatum extract standardized to 0.3-1.0% hypericin per day, divided into 2-3 doses.
Hypericum perforatum interacts with serotonergic antidepressants, increasing the risk of serotonin syndrome.
Gastrointestinal and central nervous system side effects, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, occur in some individuals.
Perform a patch test or blood test to assess individual sensitivity to Hypericum perforatum.
Hypericum perforatum may cause uterine contractions and affect fetal development.
Exposure to sunlight may cause severe skin reactions.
Due to potential interactions with antidepressants, blood thinners, and other medications, use is recommended under strict medical supervision.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Hypericum perforatum medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional to determine the best course of treatment, as St. John's Wort can interact with over 400 medications and cause adverse effects when combined with certain antidepressants, blood thinners, and immunosuppressants. Determining the correct dosage is crucial, as excessive consumption can lead to side effects such as fatigue, dizziness, and gastrointestinal issues. Monitoring for side effects is essential, as some individuals may experience allergic reactions, including skin rashes and anaphylaxis. Before starting treatment, test for allergies, as sensitivity to Hypericum perforatum can cause severe reactions, and avoid using it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as it may cause harm to the fetus or infant. Be aware of photosensitivity, as the plant's active compounds can cause skin irritation or burns when exposed to sunlight, and use Hypericum perforatum under strict medical supervision to minimize the risk of adverse effects.
identification: Hypericum perforatum is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Hypericaceae, with a woody base and a branching stem that can grow up to 1 meter in height. It has opposite, sessile, and simple leaves that are ovate to lanceolate in shape, measuring 2-4 cm in length, and have perforations that give the plant its name. The plant produces small, yellow, five-petaled flowers in clusters, usually in late spring and early summer, which are rich in nectar and attract various pollinators. Hypericum perforatum is native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa, but has been naturalized in many other parts of the world, including North America, Australia, and New Zealand, where it has become an invasive species in some areas. It typically thrives in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade conditions, and can be found growing in a variety of habitats, including meadows, woodlands, and along roadsides.
cultivation: Hypericum perforatum is a perennial herbaceous plant that grows from 10 to 90 cm in height, preferring well-drained soil and full sun to partial shade with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0. It thrives in temperate climates with moderate temperatures, requiring a minimum of 600 mm annual rainfall and a temperature range of -20 to 35°C. To promote healthy growth, Hypericum perforatum requires regular watering, especially during its first year, and can tolerate some drought but benefits from consistent moisture levels. To maximize yields, it's recommended to plant in a location with minimal competition from other vegetation, and to divide and replant the rhizomes every 3-4 years to maintain vigour and prevent decline. By providing the right conditions and care, Hypericum perforatum can be a low-maintenance and productive crop, suitable for cultivation in a variety of settings.
history: Hypericum perforatum is a plant with a long history of medicinal use dating back to ancient civilizations, including the Greeks and Romans, who used it to treat a variety of ailments, including wounds, injuries, and skin conditions, as well as to ward off evil spirits and promote mental well-being. The plant's medicinal properties were later studied by European herbalists and physicians, such as John Gerard and Nicholas Culpeper, who described its use in treating melancholy, anxiety, and other mental health conditions. The plant's efficacy was also documented in the 18th century by German physician Johann Gottfried Mikan, who isolated the active compound hypericin, which is responsible for its antiviral and antidepressant properties. Hypericum perforatum was widely used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and menstrual disorders, and was often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects. By the mid-20th century, the plant's medicinal properties had been extensively studied and it was widely recognized as a treatment for mild to moderate depression, anxiety, and sleep disorders.
